title: Rhea Seehorn Wins Golden Globe for Best Actress in TV Drama - Hollywood Reporter
text: Rhea Seehorn claimed the award for best female actor in a drama TV series at the 2026 Golden Globes for her role in the Apple TV+ series "Pluribus." This marks her first Golden Globe win and inaugural nomination in this category. Upon receiving the award, Seehorn expressed genuine surprise. She recalled meeting presenter Queen Latifah two decades prior, saying she was "so nice" at their chance encounter between jobs. The actress offered particular gratitude to series creator Vince Gilligan, stating: "Thank you for writing me the role of a lifetime. And even more than that, thank you for having belief in me." Seehorn competed against an accomplished group of nominees, including Kathy Bates ("Matlock"), Britt Lower ("Severance"), Helen Mirren ("Mobland"), Bella Ramsey ("The Last of Us"), and Keri Russell ("The Diplomat"). She praised her fellow nominees, noting she felt "astonished" to be listed among such talented performers. During backstage interviews, Seehorn acknowledged that "Pluribus" season two remains in development.
